title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot interviews Mr. Jeremy Fullerton, the legal advisor for the Dray family and the late Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith, regarding the death of a young girl named Joyce Reynolds. Fullerton provides background on the Dray family, noting that Hugo Dray became lame due to polio, and discusses the wealthy Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith, who died of heart disease two years prior.

Poirot shifts the inquiry to one of Fullerton's former employees, Leslie Ferrell (referred to by Fullerton as Leslie Firr), who was stabbed to death years ago near the Green Swan Inn in Woodley. Fullerton explains that while the police suspected the motive was jealousy involving a married woman or other girls Leslie was involved with, no one was caught due to a lack of evidence. Fullerton offers an alternative hypothesis: Leslie may have been killed by a gang after attempting to leave their illegal counterfeit transactions.

Poirot suggests that a child might have witnessed the murder of Leslie Ferrell but remained silent due to fear or the desire to keep a secret. Fullerton expresses skepticism, arguing that rumors are common and that children would likely tell their mothers. Poirot maintains that children often process traumatic or confusing sights in silence until a later event triggers their memory. He implies a potential connection between the death of Joyce Reynolds and the unsolved murder of Leslie Ferrell, though Fullerton remains dismissive of such a link.
